summary.title: Claude Code hook support
summary.what: Implemented a registered Claude Code hooks agent that installs and uninstalls mainline-managed entries in project-local .claude/settings.json, parses Claude Code lifecycle payloads, and emits Claude Code-compatible hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext for SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit. Stop, SubagentStop, PreCompact, and SessionEnd are mapped as observer lifecycle events without semantic Mainline automation.
summary.why: Claude Code exposes native hooks through settings.json and can carry agent-visible additional context for supported events. Adding a Claude Code agent gives Mainline the same context-provider workflow already available for Cursor and Codex while preserving the boundary that hooks do not decide when to start, append, seal, or judge intents.
summary.user_goal: Create a new branch and add support for Claude Code hooks, then complete the normal Mainline commit/seal/push flow.

decisions.chose: Install SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SubagentStop, PreCompact, and SessionEnd.
decisions.rationale: These cover Mainline's session, turn, subagent, compaction, and end-of-session lifecycle taxonomy without touching tool-use policy hooks.

decisions.chose: Render additionalContext only for SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit.
decisions.rationale: Those are the useful model-visible context points for Mainline state; Stop/SubagentStop/PreCompact/SessionEnd remain webhook/observer signals and never synthesize semantic workflow actions.

decisions.chose: Read-modify-write .claude/settings.json, removing only mainline-managed claudecode commands and preserving unrelated top-level settings and user hook entries.
decisions.rationale: Claude Code settings commonly include permissions, env, model, and user hooks; the installer must converge safely without clobbering those keys.

decisions.chose: Change the common per-prompt context heading from Codex-specific wording to neutral hook wording.
decisions.rationale: Claude Code and Codex now share RenderTurnStartContext, so the text must not imply the wrong agent.

rejected.alternative: Leave Claude Code as an unregistered placeholder package
rejected.reason: The requested support requires the agent to register with the hooks registry and appear in list/install/status flows.

rejected.alternative: Use Claude Code hooks to enforce the full Mainline flow automatically
rejected.reason: Start/append/seal/check still require agent semantic judgment and remain outside hook code.
That would be unsafe for user permissions, env, model settings, and existing hook configuration.

risks: Claude Code hook protocol and hookSpecificOutput behavior can change; this implementation follows the current official hook shape but may need updates if Claude Code changes event names or output contracts.
Project-local .claude/settings.json is ignored in this repository, so installing hooks is a local setup step and not represented by committed config.
SessionStart runs sync and can update local Mainline cache state; this remains mechanical but can make hook startup dependent on git/network latency.
followups: Consider installing SubagentStart later if Claude Code subagent context injection becomes important for Mainline-aware delegated work.
Consider adding a shared helper package for JSON hook settings mutation if more agents use the same grouped command hook shape.

architectural_claims: Claude Code is now a first-class hooks.Agent registered through the CLI blank import and hooks registry.
Claude Code install/uninstall is scoped to mainline-managed command entries in .claude/settings.json and preserves unrelated user settings.
Claude Code hook rendering reuses the existing Dispatcher context-provider model rather than adding semantic start/append/seal automation.
The shared per-prompt context renderer is agent-neutral so Codex and Claude Code can both use it safely.
behavioral_changes: mainline hooks list-agents now includes Claude Code (claudecode).
mainline hooks install --agent claudecode writes six Claude Code lifecycle hook entries into .claude/settings.json.
mainline hooks uninstall --agent claudecode removes only mainline-managed Claude Code hook commands.
Claude Code SessionStart hook output includes full Mainline session-start context via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext.
Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook output includes lightweight per-prompt Mainline context via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext.
Claude Code Stop, SubagentStop, PreCompact, and SessionEnd dispatch normalized lifecycle events without stdout context output.

api_changes.signature: UserPromptSubmit additionalContext text says Hooks refreshed lightweight Mainline state

data_model_changes.name: .claude/settings.json mainline-managed hook entries
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Only written when users run hooks install for claudecode; uninstall removes mainline-managed entries and preserves unrelated settings.

security_implications: Installer preserves existing Claude Code permissions and user hook settings rather than rewriting them.
Wrapper commands are generated from fixed hook ids and optional configured binary path, with fail-soft guards so missing binaries do not break Claude Code sessions.
No user prompt content is sent to webhooks by this change; prompt text is only held in the normalized local hook event for dispatch.
migration_notes: No repository data migration is required.
Users who want Claude Code integration should run mainline hooks install --agent claudecode; existing Cursor/Codex integrations are unchanged.
